channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pope Leo XIV Advocates Diplomacy and Humanitarian Efforts Amid Conflicts in Israel and Ukraine
item.description: On Good Friday, Pope Leo XIV emphasized the need for renewed diplomatic efforts to resolve conflicts in the Middle East and Ukraine through separate phone conversations with Israeli President Isaac He...

item.title: French container ship granted passage through Strait of Hormuz amid Iran conflict
item.description: A French container ship has become the first major western vessel to navigate safely through the Strait of Hormuz since the outbreak of the Iran war five weeks ago. The ship, operated by CMA CGM and o...

item.title: Military neutralizes bandits and arrests impostors posing as security personnel in Plateau State
item.description: Troops of the Joint Task Force (JTF) Operation Enduring Peace (OPEP) carried out successful operations in Plateau State, Nigeria, resulting in the death of three suspected bandits and the arrest of tw...

item.title: Critique of Media Reporting on Forest Fires and Other Events
item.description: The article discusses how media coverage often omits the human or deliberate causes behind disasters, using forest fires in northern mountains of Spain as the main example. The author points out that ...

item.title: Dangote Refinery Clarifies May Crude Allocation Amid Conflicting Reports
item.description: Confusion has arisen over the amount of crude oil the Dangote Petroleum Refinery will receive from the Nigerian National Petroleum Company (NNPC) in May. While some reports suggested an increase from ...

item.title: California Toddler Survives Coyote Attack in Driveway
item.description: In Southern California, a four-year-old boy named Solomon survived a terrifying encounter with a coyote in his grandparents' driveway. Security camera footage captured the incident, showing the coyote...

item.title: Government reduces petrol levy by Rs80, bringing price to Rs378 per litre
item.description: On Friday, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif announced a significant reduction in the petrol levy, cutting it by Rs80 per litre. This step brought the petrol price down to Rs378 per litre, effective from ...

item.title: US and Iran compete to locate downed F-15 pilot amid bounty, one crew member rescued
item.description: On 3 April 2026, a US F-15E fighter jet was shot down over southern Iran, prompting a tense search for the two-person crew. One crew member was swiftly rescued in a daring Special Forces operation inv...

item.title: Sindh Government Provides Rs2,000 Petrol Subsidy for Registered Motorcycles
item.description: The Sindh government has announced a petrol subsidy of Rs2,000 for each registered motorcycle to ease the burden of skyrocketing fuel prices. Petrol has recently surged to Rs458.41 per litre while hig...

item.title: Seplat Energy staff go on indefinite strike over pay and welfare issues
item.description: Workers for Seplat Energy don start indefinite strike as matter of pay and welfare no settle. The strike dey affect main operations, including production reporting and export work, but essential servi...

item.title: Meta Suspends Projects with Data Vendor Mercor Amid Security Breach
item.description: Meta has temporarily halted all projects with Mercor, a major data vendor for AI model training, following a security breach that potentially exposed proprietary data. The incident, confirmed by Merco...

item.title: Cleric raises concerns over INEC voter card revalidation process
item.description: The Chief Imam of Al-Furqan Mosque for Kano, Dr Bashir Aliyu Umar, don yan serious wahala wey fit happen if Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) continue with dem plan to revalidate all vo...

item.title: King Charles and David Beckham Collaborate on 'Curious Garden' for Chelsea Flower Show
item.description: King Charles, 77, recently welcomed Sir David Beckham, along with Royal Horticultural Society (RHS) Ambassador Alan Titchmarsh and garden designer Frances Tophill, to Highgrove Gardens in Gloucestersh...

item.title: Christian Nationalist Influence in US Government Raises Concerns About Military Actions and Religious Bias
item.description: The article discusses the growing influence of Christian nationalist ideology within the U.S. government, particularly under Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth. Hegseth has held prayer services at the ...

item.title: Rory McIlroy Discusses His Masters Title, Motivation, and Future Ambitions
item.description: Rory McIlroy, fresh from his historic Masters victory in 2025, insists that he remains fully motivated despite achieving the rare feat of completing a career grand slam. At 36, he feels physically fit...

item.title: Many Seniors Return to Work Through Gig Jobs Due to Financial Needs
item.description: A growing number of older Americans are returning to the workforce after having retired, a trend known as 'unretiring.' Rising living costs, inadequate retirement savings, and a desire to remain activ...

item.title: Omar Aqil Transforms Digital Overload into Playful Self-Portraits
item.description: Pakistani designer Omar Aqil has created a series of self-portraits that reinterpret the concept of identity through a mix of digital clutter and creative composition. Instead of traditional portraits...

item.title: US Fighter Jets Downed Over Iran Challenge Claims of Air Superiority
item.description: The recent downing of two U.S. fighter jets over Iran marks a significant development in an already unpopular conflict. While one crew member has been rescued and treated, the status of the other rema...

item.title: Artemis II Begins Journey Toward Moon After Earth Orbit Departure
item.description: The Artemis II mission has successfully left Earth's orbit and begun its four-day journey to the moon. Astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen are aboard the Orion sp...

item.title: US and Israeli Forces Conduct Search for Downed F-15 Pilot Over Iran
item.description: An American F-15 fighter jet was shot down over Iranian territory, prompting a joint search-and-rescue operation by U.S. and Israeli forces for one missing crew member. This incident marks the first t...
